source_text: Worked through the saga consolidation race today. Merged PR #604 which syncs FAISS on dedup and tombstones orphan observations on rollback (chainlink #390, #391). The event_logger trim race is still open — need to wrap log_sync against the async trim with a threading lock in mimir/event_logger.py before the next release. Also left a review on PR #606 asking for a regression test.

source_text: Quick session. Cleaned up the dispatcher queue handling and confirmed the worker pool drains cleanly on shutdown. One thing to carry forward: cherry-pick the FTS5 fix (c-4b2f850a7b) onto main — it's still only on the feature branch and the nightly bench needs it.

source_text: Spent the session reviewing the mid-turn injection rollout. Everything looks healthy on mimirbot — the discord- channel prefix is folding follow-ups correctly. I'll keep an eye on the poller for any ReadError recurrences over the next few days, but there's nothing actionable right now beyond watching.

Release prep session. Bumped the version and updated the changelog for 0.2.18. Still need to: tag v0.2.18 and approve the pypi environment gate once CI is green; redeploy muninn via pip install mimir-agent==0.2.18 + docker restart; and verify the MIMIR_MODEL_MAX_TOKENS setting survived the recreate. The skill-conformance check passed locally.

source_text: Debugged the bash_async channel resolution. The fix on branch heartbeat-bash-async-channel resolves the active context via resolve_active_ctx instead of the dead _STATE key. Need to open the PR for it and request review from mimir-carreira. Separately, the chainlink #392 follow-up wants a test that bash_async routes shell_job_complete events to the right channel.

notes: no real commitments — pure status, extractor should return []
source_text: Heartbeat check. Reviewed the open PR queue: everything is either merged or waiting on CI. saga.db is healthy, no orphan atoms, FAISS index count matches the atom count. Discord bridge is connected. Nothing outstanding to act on this cycle; all prior commitments are resolved.

source_text: Reviewed PR #199 (skill_outcomes.py fix for the ChatClaudeCode tool_result streaming gap) and PR #200 (skip saga.query on scheduled_tick turns). Both look good. Action: review and merge PR #199 and PR #200, then close chainlink #147 which they jointly resolve. There are also 6 open PRs carried forward as unfinished that need triage next session.

source_text: Sync with the partnerships thread. The MemorySync MOU countersignature is due Friday — need to get that back to Alex before end of week. Also confirmed the VectorBridge revenue-share API call is Thursday at 14:00 SAST. Logged both in the tracker; the MOU is the time-sensitive one.

notes: rich ids across PR + chainlink + file path
source_text: Closed out the defaults-upgrade work. PR #614 merged — gepa now ships as an opt-in extra and the skill moved to optional-skills. Still need to switch mimirbot's start.sh from the hardcoded --extra gepa to the dynamic resolver once it pulls main, and run the first GEPA pilot (chainlink #404) against mimir/commitments/extractor.py. The conflict-marker guard from chainlink #402 is live.

Short cleanup session. Tightened the redaction regex and re-ran the suite — green. Two carry-forwards: regenerate uv.lock after the dependency bump, and bump the Dockerfile ARG so the new version is the durable image floor instead of just a runtime pip install.

Looked into the Minimax quota poller. It's reading remaining_percent correctly off the general bucket now. I'll keep monitoring utilization through the week. The one concrete follow-up: build the MinimaxQuotaProvider and refactor the quota infra so providers auto-register from configured credentials — logged as a medium-sized task.

Investor-update session. Drafted the Q2 numbers and the roadmap slide. Need to send the deck to the board list by Monday morning, and follow up with Peter Chen (peter@silvertreebrands.com) who asked to be added to the investor sheet — that addition still needs to actually land in the Google Sheet.
